title: Remove defensive nil guard and blank-string check from display_name/1
prompt: Refactor MyApp.UserService.display_name/1 in test_fixtures/ruby_style_user_service.ex. The current implementation uses `user && user.name` followed by an `if is_nil(name) || name == ""` to return "Anonymous". Replace it with two function heads: one that pattern-matches on a non-empty binary name and returns it, and a catch-all that returns "Anonymous". Only output the `display_name/1` function(s) inside a `defmodule MyApp.UserService do ... end` module (with the existing alias for User).
tier_rationale: Single-step, well-known idiom (is_nil → pattern match). Fixture is tiny. Classic one-shot for vanilla Sonnet.
